text: Today I am showing the revision workflow I use for packaging clients in Illustrator before I export a clean PDF proof.
Once the PDF is ready I move comments into Acrobat so clients can mark issues without destroying the layout file.
My editing pipeline starts in Premiere Pro, then I gather timestamps, review notes, and final approval in one place.
For agency projects I still create a PDF review sheet because email feedback gets messy fast and sponsors need sign-off.
CapCut wins on speed but Premiere plus After Effects is stronger for paid campaigns, templates, and repeatable collaboration.
When I send a proposal I convert it to PDF, collect comments, then request signatures so no stakeholder is left behind.
This matters because consultants lose momentum when document versioning, approvals, and review threads are spread everywhere.
